codeReview.summary: The double-buffer implementation is well-designed and correctly integrated into _decode_step_cached_stream() and _decode_step_serial() decode paths. The core mechanism (alternating buffers each layer, removing wait at layer start, swapping after each allreduce) is sound. The minimal buffer swap test passed. However, the implementation only covers 2 of the 7 dispatch paths, and the full TP=4 correctness test was not completed due to time constraints.

issues.description: _decode_step_c_dispatch() does not support double-buffer mode. When both _double_buffer_enabled and _c_dispatch_enabled are true, the double-buffer flag is ignored. This is acceptable as C dispatch is a lower-level optimization, but should be documented.
_decode_step_graph() and _decode_step_global_graph() do not support double-buffer mode. These dispatch paths use HIP graph capture which has different synchronization requirements.

In _decode_step_cached_stream(), after all layers complete, the code reads from e0.d_hidden but should read from the correct buffer. Since buffers are swapped after each layer, after 64 layers the buffer state depends on whether num_layers is even or odd. For 64 layers (even), the final swap leaves d_hidden pointing to d_hidden_A. This is correct but the logic is implicit.

Full TP=4 correctness test (test_double_buffer_correctness) was not executed. The minimal test passed, validating buffer alternation. The full test requires 10+ minutes to load 64 layers and build dispatch cache. Recommended: run full test before considering this feature production-ready.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Double-buffer mode only provides throughput benefit when combined with set_stream_overlap_dispatch(True). Without stream overlap, it just alternates buffers without hiding latency. This should be documented more prominently.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: DOUBLE_BUFFER_IMPLEMENTATION.md notes this, but the set_double_buffer_enabled() method docstring doesn't mention the dependency.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The dispatch path priority order in decode_step() determines which optimizations take effect. When multiple optimizations are enabled, higher-priority paths (graph, C dispatch) take precedence, which can silently disable lower-priority optimizations like double-buffer.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/inference/tp_engine.py:2052-2069 shows the priority chain: global_graph > graph > c_dispatch > batched_allreduce > cached+stream > cached > stream > threaded > serial. Double-buffer only works in cached+stream and serial paths.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The kernel-optimization-worker skill procedure assumes tests complete quickly. Full TP=4 tests take 10+ minutes due to weight loading and dispatch cache building. Skill should include guidance for long-running GPU tests or suggest lightweight test modes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker ran minimal test instead of full TP=4 test due to timeout constraints. skillFeedback.deviations confirms this. The skill's Step 6 (Run Correctness Tests) doesn't mention timeout planning.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The _allreduce_residual_double_buffer() method supports multiple allreduce implementations (kernel P2P, fused P2P, ring, standard P2P, fast_ar C extension, Python fallback). All write to d_hidden_write buffer. This is well-designed with proper fallback chain.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/inference/tp_engine.py:2941-3040 shows the complete fallback chain.

summary: PASS with non-blocking issues. The double-buffer implementation is correct and follows the specified design. The core mechanism (even layers read A/write B, odd layers read B/write A, swap after each layer) is properly implemented in _decode_step_cached_stream() and _decode_step_serial(). The minimal buffer swap test passed. Key gaps: (1) Full TP=4 correctness test not executed due to time constraints - recommend running before production use. (2) Double-buffer only works in cached+stream and serial dispatch paths, not in C dispatch or graph dispatch paths. (3) The feature requires set_stream_overlap_dispatch(True) for actual throughput benefit. Documentation is good with DOUBLE_BUFFER_IMPLEMENTATION.md providing clear implementation details and usage instructions.
